concerns.text: FR-003 contains an incomplete arXiv reference: 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/[identifier]'. The placeholder '[identifier]' must be replaced with the actual arXiv identifier for the Seifert circle count algorithm source before implementation.

FR-006 specifies effect size measures as 'Cohen's d for group comparisons' but SC-009 specifies 'mean difference, variance ratio' for alternating vs. non-alternating group comparisons. Cohen's d is a standardized mean difference, while mean difference is the raw difference. These metrics should be aligned for consistency.

FR-002 uses 'data_quality_flags' for records failing data quality conditions, while FR-009 uses 'missing_invariant_flags' for records where invariants are not computable. These may be different flag types, but the distinction is not clearly explained. Consider clarifying whether these are the same flag or different flag categories.

SC-001 states 'required invariant fields must have high completeness' without specifying a quantitative threshold. SC-013 defines <5% null percentage but SC-001 should explicitly state the same threshold for consistency and testability.
SC-010 uses 'high match threshold' while FR-003 and SC-005 specify '≥90%'. Should use consistent quantified language for verifiability.
FR-002 states 'null percentage below a minimal threshold' without specifying the value. SC-013 clarifies this as <5% but FR-002 should reference the explicit threshold for standalone verifiability.

FR-006 states 'statistical significance threshold of p < 0.05 is appropriate' but Assumptions and SC-009 clarify census data makes p-values descriptive not inferential. Should clarify p-values are for reporting convention only, not inferential claims.

Idea specifies 80/20 stratified train/test split and ANOVA for model comparison. Spec removes both citing 'census data' rationale (FR-005, Assumptions). This drifts from predictive validation to descriptive analysis without explicit idea-stage approval.

Idea specifies cross-validated R-squared for model selection. Spec uses goodness-of-fit metrics (R², AIC/BIC, MAE) without cross-validation. This removes the validation mechanism the idea explicitly required.

Idea states 'validation target (hyperbolic volume) is measured independently from crossing number and braid index.' Spec treats hyperbolic volume as tabulated data without explicit validation requirement against independent reference.

Spec introduces multiple methodology shifts (train/test removal, ANOVA→descriptive, cross-validation removal) documented as 'intentional' but these were not decided at idea stage. This constitutes scope drift on research methodology.

Spec acknowledges braid index ≤ crossing number constraint but does not explicitly address multicollinearity in joint regression. Predictors are inherently correlated by mathematical inequality. Analysis plan should include variance inflation factor (VIF) assessment or alternative methods (e.g., ridge regression, principal component analysis) to handle correlated predictors. Current design may produce unstable coefficient estimates.

Assumption states 'Statistical significance threshold of p < 0.05 is appropriate for this exploratory analysis' while simultaneously stating p-values 'must not support inferential claims.' This is contradictory. If p-values are for convention only, the significance threshold is irrelevant. Clarify: either remove p-value discussion entirely for census data, or specify p-values are for reporting convention without inferential interpretation.

Assumption states 'Sample size documentation for stratified groups will be provided to confirm adequate sample sizes for descriptive reporting.' For census data (all prime knots ≤13 crossings), sample size is fixed and known, not a matter of documentation. Rephrase to reflect that stratified subgroup counts will be reported as descriptive facts, not power adequacy assessments.

FR-003 still contains incomplete arXiv reference: 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/' without identifier. Prior concern internal_consistency-53317216 claimed this was fixed to 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0303273' but the actual spec text I'm reviewing still shows the incomplete placeholder. The revision was not applied to the version under review.

User Story 4 Acceptance Scenario 2 states 'the record is flagged with data_quality_flags' for missing invariant data, but FR-009 specifies 'missing_invariant_flags' for invariants not computable from available diagram representations. These are distinct flag categories per FR-002/FR-009 distinction; User Story 4 should use 'missing_invariant_flags' to align with FR-009.

SC-001 states 'Total prime knots at crossing number ≤13: the established count (source: OEIS A002863)' while Assumptions section states 'Total prime knots at crossing number ≤13: 9988 (source: OEIS A002863)'. SC-001 should include the explicit number (9988) for measurability and consistency with the Assumptions section.

responses.response: Added explicit threshold value to FR-002 for standalone verifiability.

Prior concern internal_consistency-53317216 claimed this was fixed to 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0303273' but the actual spec text I'm reviewing still shows the incomplete placeholder. The revision was not applied to the version under review.

User Story 4 Acceptance Scenario 2 states 'the record is flagged with data_quality_flags' for missing invariant data, but FR-009 specifies 'missing_invariant_flags' for invariants not computable from available diagram representations. These are distinct flag categories per FR-002/FR-009 distinction; User Story 4 should use 'missing_invariant_flags' to align with FR-009.

SC-001 states 'Total prime knots at crossing number ≤13: the established count (source: OEIS A002863)' while Assumptions section states 'Total prime knots at crossing number ≤13: 9988 (source: OEIS A002863)'. SC-001 should include the explicit number (9988) for measurability and consistency with the Assumptions section.

concerns.text: FR-003 still contains incomplete arXiv reference: 'source: arXiv preprint' without identifier. Prior concern internal_consistency-3b2016d3 claimed this was fixed to a complete identifier but the actual spec text I'm reviewing still shows the incomplete placeholder. The revision was not applied to the version under review.

FR-002 states 'null percentage below an acceptable threshold' without specifying the threshold value. SC-013 clarifies <5%, but the FR itself should reference or include this quantification for standalone verifiability.

FR-003 states validation against KnotInfo requires 'a high standard' as the pass/fail threshold. This is vague—SC-010 clarifies ≥90% match threshold, but FR-003 should reference this explicitly or include the quantification.

Algorithm validation threshold ambiguity: FR-003 and SC-010 use '≥90%' for both (1) reference coverage requirement AND (2) match threshold. This creates logical circularity — is validation skipped if coverage <90%, or if match <90%? Clarify these are distinct thresholds with separate skip conditions.

Validation source independence: FR-013 validates Knot Atlas hyperbolic volume against KnotInfo. Both databases may share underlying data sources/methodologies. Document whether Knot Atlas and KnotInfo are independently derived to ensure validation is not circular.

responses.response: Fixed the incomplete arXiv reference in FR-003 by replacing 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/' with the complete identifier 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0303273' as confirmed by verified facts.

responses.what_changed: User Story 4 Acceptance Scenario 2: corrected flag type to match FR-009 specification

responses.response: Updated SC-001 to include the explicit prime knot count (9988) at crossing number ≤13, consistent with the Assumptions section and verified against OEIS A002863.

The revision was not applied to the version under review.

FR-002 states 'null percentage below an acceptable threshold' without specifying the threshold value. SC-013 clarifies <5%, but the FR itself should reference or include this quantification for standalone verifiability.

FR-003 states validation against KnotInfo requires 'a high standard' as the pass/fail threshold. This is vague—SC-010 clarifies ≥90% match threshold, but FR-003 should reference this explicitly or include the quantification.

Algorithm validation threshold ambiguity: FR-003 and SC-010 use '≥90%' for both (1) reference coverage requirement AND (2) match threshold. This creates logical circularity — is validation skipped if coverage <90%, or if match <90%? Clarify these are distinct thresholds with separate skip conditions.

Validation source independence: FR-013 validates Knot Atlas hyperbolic volume against KnotInfo. Both databases may share underlying data sources/methodologies. Document whether Knot Atlas and KnotInfo are independently derived to ensure validation is not circular.

concerns.text: FR-003 still contains incomplete arXiv placeholder: 'source: math/[arXiv identifier], https://arxiv.org/abs/math/[arXiv identifier]' for Seifert circle count algorithm. The reviser claimed this was fixed to 'math/0303273' but the actual spec text under review still shows the placeholder '[arXiv identifier]' rather than the resolved identifier. This is the same unresolved concern from prior review (internal_consistency-a6248940).

Spec removes train/test split (80/20), ANOVA, and cross-validation from original idea methodology. While spec justifies this as 'census data constraints,' the original idea's research question asks about 'predictive power' which implies hold-out validation. This methodology shift changes the research question from predictive validation to descriptive analysis. Need explicit confirmation that this drift is acceptable for the research goals.

FR-003 adds computation of additional invariants (arc index, Seifert circle count, bridge number) as 'exploratory extension for Phase 2+'. Original idea explicitly states 'No additional invariants in Phase 1' as a methodology boundary. While spec defers to Phase 2+, this adds scope beyond the original idea's explicit boundary. Confirm this extension aligns with project goals.
Redundant citation repetition: 'source: OEIS A002863, https://oeis.org/A002863' appears 4+ times in the same paragraph. This is a documentation quality issue that should be cleaned up.

responses.response: Replaced vague 'high standard' phrasing with explicit ≥90% match threshold in FR-003 for standalone verifiability.

responses.response: Clarified the distinction between coverage threshold and match threshold with separate skip conditions. Coverage <90% triggers skip; match <90% triggers fail. These are now explicitly differentiated.
